item.title: Nintendo Updated Super Mario Galaxy 2 After 16 Years And Fans Are Confused, Mostly
item.content:encoded:
Sixteen years after it was first released, Super Mario Galaxy 2 got a random update last night on Switch, and on top of some bug fixes, it added a new storybook segment about Rosalina. Given some of the changes The Super Mario Galaxy Movie made to the character’s backstory and Nintendo’s messaging on the matter, fans were expecting this update to finally draw a familial line between Rosalina and another character directly in the old Wii game itself. But now that fans have actually seen the story in full, they’re mostly just confused and arguing about what it all means.

Players can unlock the storybook “Epilogue” after clearing any galaxy and earning a Power Star, but if you don’t have time to boot the game back up, you can just watch it here:

The story shows Rosalina having “a premonition that a new star would be born,” then watching two shooting stars orbit one another. As she cries at the sight of them, a young girl comes up to her and tells her not to, and that her mother is waiting for her tears to dry. The young girl says she wants to find her “special someone” like the two stars found one another, and Rosalina tells her, “The strongest force in all the universe is a heart that never gives up.” 

It’s really not much, but Rosalina speaking with a young blonde girl sure sounds like something out of The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, which made Rosalina and Princess Peach canonically sisters. But some fans aren’t so sure.

I'm seeing some confusion about the new epilogue chapter "Journey" of Galaxy 2's storybook.

The girl isn't Peach, it's kid Rosalina. Rosalina is reflecting on her past, they show this symbolically by having her talk to her past self.

The earrings? Most likely her mother's. pic.twitter.com/LvTLfWw6o6

— 🌌🌠galactic✨glamour✨girl🌠🌌 (@galact1cglamour) April 30, 2026

One interpretation is that the child is Rosalina, and that the adult version is speaking to her inner child as she wistfully dreams of finding her “special someone.” The two shooting stars have also alternatively been taken as symbols for Rosalina and Peach, as they are said to be blue and purple, which are almost the two’s signature colors, though Peach is more heavily associated with pink. What’s more likely is that these are meant to represent Lumas Lubba and Mari, who are featured in the storybook.

Overall, it’s kind of a nothing burger. Leave it to Nintendo to update a 16-year-old game in the middle of the night with something small but vague enough to cause tons of speculation. Nintendo legend Shigeru Miyamoto said he would like for the movies and games to feel more connected moving forward, and for the games to adhere to the worldbuilding of the films. So fans are pretty much bracing themselves for Nintendo to make Rosalina and Peach sisters in the games. For now, however, this random Super Mario Galaxy 2 update doesn’t seem to actually do that.

“I knew it was never gonna have anything to do with the movie, but it would have been REAL funny if it did and made them siblings in the games,” a Redditor wrote. “The discourse would have been the stuff of legend.”

Despite the resemblance, Rosalina was never confirmed to be related to Peach in the games. Early in the original Galaxy game’s development, Nintendo considered tying the characters together, but this never actually came to be until The Super Mario Galaxy Movie confirmed they were sisters in the film universe.



https://kotaku.com/super-mario-galaxy-2-storybook-update-rosalina-peach-2000692009

item.title: Amid $100 Rumors, Take-Two Won’t Commit To GTA 6 Price Yet
item.content:encoded:
Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick addressed the oft-discussed rumors about Grand Theft Auto 6’s pricing. While there were no definitive answers, Zelnick provided a look into the mindset the company is approaching the release with.

The comments were made at the IICON conference for gaming executives. Primarily, he emphasized the importance of ensuring that a game’s value exceeds its price point. Additionally, he noted that game pricing has generally decreased over the years, with most games priced between $60 and $70 for over a decade.

Take-Two CEO Won’t Commit to a Price for GTA 6

During his comments at IICON, Strauss Zelnick stated that he believes consumers will pay for the value they get, but as service providers, they have to overload their products with value.

“Consumers pay for the value that you bring to them, and our job is to charge way way way less of the value delivery,” Zelnick said (via IGN). “How you feel about something you buy is the intersection of the thing itself and what you pay for. Consumers need to feel like the thing itself is amazing and the price they were charged was fair for what they got.”

With such intense hype surrounding GTA 6, Zelnick admitted he’s terrified of the idea of measuring the game’s success.

“What we think about is making the most spectacular piece of entertainment on Earth, in history – and it’s a pretty daunting challenge. If we do that, and if we’re of service to our customers, then the upside will take care of itself,” he said.

He also addressed the release date, stating that it would remain the previously announced November date. Learn more about that here. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Resident Evil Movie’s First Trailer is Live
item.content:encoded:
Zach Cregger’s Resident Evil movie has received its first teaser trailer, building hype for the September 2026 release.

The first trailer for the Resident Evil movie was showcased at CinemaCon, with the creators going all-in on gore. The trailer received a pretty good response from the audience at the event, and now it is finally time for the general public to see it in action. Interested readers can check out the trailer embedded below:

Resident Evil Movie Trailer Provides a Small Glimpse of What’s to Come

The new trailer for the Resident Evil movie begins with Austin Abrams, known for his role in Weapons, searching for a phone in a deserted house located in the middle of a snowy field. After he manages to access the phone, he calls his girlfriend to inform her that “some things have happened” and that he won’t be seeing her anytime soon. From that moment on, the trailer’s tone shifts dramatically, featuring intense action sequences and the zombies Abrams will encounter.

The trailer provides players with a brief look at the various types of zombies featured in the movie. Among them are a massive, blobby monster lurking in the sewers and hordes of zombies attacking from multiple directions, as well as various blood-filled environments. As the trailer progresses, it intensifies, showing Austin running through a narrow lane while zombies fall from rooftops around him like explosive body bombs. This essentially captures the level of horror and gore that director Cregger aims to deliver.

In other news, Resident Evil Requiem developers have provided the first update on the upcoming story DLC. Additionally, the game’s producer has hinted at a different mini-game arriving soon. What are your thoughts on Resident Evil movie’s first trailer? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Star Wars: Galactic Racer’s release date receiving an official announcement. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Dinosaur stealth survival horror game Wilderdark announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Demo due out on May 7.

Monstrum series developer Team Junkfish has announced Wilderdark, a first-person stealth survival horror game where you must navigate a strange island inhabited by ectoparasitic dinosaurs and organisms. It will launch for PC via Steam in 2026. A demo will launch on May 7.

“We’ve been holding onto Wilderdark as a well-kept secret for over three years, and we’re super excited to finally shine a spotlight on it,” said Team Junkfish senior marketing manager Ellie Gibbs in a press release. “After years of quietly building it behind-the-scenes, it feels amazing to share something that marks our return to the kind of horror our community has been asking for. The demo is just the beginning…step in next week and see what’s been lurking in the dark.”

  • ❮
  • ❯

Here is an overview of the game, via Team Junkfish:

About

Wilderdark is a survival horror exploration game where you find yourself on an unnatural island infected with a mysterious ectoparasitic organism which is corrupting the dinosaurs and plantlife. Sent in by the organization you work for in search of immortality, you are tasked with documenting the island’s ecosystem, collecting samples and unraveling the fate of previously vanished expeditions—including one led by your own parents.

In Wilderdark, players find themselves crash-landed on a hostile island with only minimal resources to survive. Every step matters in this tense and atmospheric world, where stealth is key and even the smallest sound can attract the attention of deadly apex predators. The island is teeming with unfamiliar and dangerous lifeforms, including grotesque, parasite-infected dinosaurs. To uncover the truth, players will need to carefully observe their surroundings, document discoveries through photography, and craft essential tools from whatever they can find. It’s a constant balancing act: use resources wisely, stay out of sight, and think one step ahead of the island’s relentless hunters.

Explore and Investigate

A crash landing on the island means starting with minimal resources and tools. Stealth is required to survive the island filled with danger, and piece together the truth through environmental storytelling, photographic documentation, and crafted tools. Careful use of resources is the only way to hide and survive the hunt of apex predators.

Ectoparasites

Much is unknown of their origin and their effects on organic life, including humans. One wrong move could mean the end or the start of something unimaginable.

Can you escape the fate that has fallen so many and live to tell a tale?

Watch the announcement trailer below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Cinematic Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/dinosaur-stealth-survival-horror-game-wilderdark-announced-for-pc

item.title: Atomfall TV Show in the Works from Two Brothers Pictures
item.content:encoded:
Rebellion and production company Two Brothers Pictures have announced that Atomfall is getting a TV show adaptation, and that it'll be worked on by a producer-writer duo who've worked on the likes of Fleabag and Assassin.

As reported by Eurogamer, the Atomfall TV show will be written by Harry and Jack Williams, whose previous credits include the aforementioned shows, as well as Baptiste and The Missing. Executive producer Alex Mercer, meanwhile, has worked on shows like Doctor Who and Maya.

The Williams brothers say that adapting Atomfall's "distinctive British tone and setting" for TV has been "a real joy", and that they're excited about "expanding this strange, unsettling story for television". Rebellion heads Jason and Chris Kingsley, meanwhile, say they're "delighted" by the partnership.

We don't yet know whether the Atomfall TV show will be directly adapting the story of the game or whether it'll choose to tell a more character-centric story; after all, the game is a rather freeform experience that involves organic exploration and discovery, which is a trickier proposition for a show with a linear narrative.

Either way, Atomfall being adapted for TV makes a lot of sense when considering the game's success. Having reached a million players within its first weekend (no doubt bolstered by a day one Game Pass release), the survival adventure stands as developer Rebellion's most successful launch ever.

If you haven't played Atomfall, it's a first-person survival game inspired by classic British media like The Prisoner, Doctor Who, and The Quatermass Experiment, as well as videogames like BioShock and Fallout: New Vegas.

The game sees you exploring a quaint British village and its surrounding countryside in the wake of an alternate-history version of the real-life Windscale nuclear disaster, and it lets you off the leash to explore and discover things on your own impressively early on in proceedings.

Since last year's launch, Atomfall has received two major story expansions, Wicked Isle and The Red Strain, both of which expand on some of the factions and enemies you'll encounter in the base game.

You can check out Atomfall right now on PC, PlayStation, and Xbox, and you can read our very own Erren's review of the game right here.

item.title: NACON Connect Being Held Next Week
item.content:encoded:
NACON is officially holding its Connect event in one week, as of this writing. A new press release confirms that the event will be held on May 7. This comes at a turbulent time for NACON and the video game industry as a whole. The presentation is set to focus on the company’s new projects across its accessories branch and the video games it will publish.

The event was originally scheduled for March but was delayed after the company announced it was filing for emergency insolvency. At the time, the company confidently stated that this would not be the end for them. However, the difficulties have continued to pile on. Recently, it was reported that three of NACON’s studios, Spiders, Kylotonn, and Cyanide, would also be filing for insolvency. Learn more about that at this link.

More on the Upcoming NACON Connect Event

The upcoming NACON Connect Event on May 7 is slated to feature a number of titles, including more details on the recently announced Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish. The presentation will be streamed on the company’s official YouTube and Twitch channels at 1:00 p.m. ET.

“On the program for the evening: announcements of ambitious games, a preview of upcoming innovations for its range of accessories, and exclusive gameplay sequences for anticipated titles such as The Mound, Edge of Memories, Endurance Motorsport Series, and the very recently announced Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish,” the company said in a press release.

Insider Gaming will provide full coverage of the event on May 7. For more NACON news, learn about the cancellation of several physical releases the company was planning on.

Are you excited about the upcoming event at all? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
